steps.title: Use the decay pattern
steps.content: In a model written as $y=a(b)^x$, the value of $b$ is the multiplier each time $x$ increases by $1$. A decreasing exponential model has a positive multiplier less than $1$.

steps.title: Find the only possible choice
steps.content: Among the choices, only $0.83$ is greater than $0$ and less than $1$. The other choices are greater than $1$, which would make the exponential model increase. The answer is A.
